proposed_hypotheses.text: In patients with a BRCA2 mutation (brca2_mutation=1), receiving the PARP inhibitor olaparib (treatment_olaparib=1) is associated with longer pfs_months than not receiving olaparib; this benefit is larger in BRCA2-mutant patients than in BRCA2-wildtype patients (positive interaction).

analyses.result_summary: OLS with interaction: olaparib effect in BRCA2+ = +1.545 mo, in BRCA2- = -0.073 mo; interaction coefficient = +1.619 (p=4.33e-61). Group sizes n(olap+,BRCA2+)=529, n(olap+,BRCA2-)=4569, n(olap-,BRCA2+)=4467, n(olap-,BRCA2-)=40435.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In patients with MSI-high tumors (msi_high=1), receiving pembrolizumab (treatment_pembrolizumab=1) is associated with longer pfs_months relative to not receiving pembrolizumab; this benefit is larger in MSI-high patients than in MSI-stable patients (positive interaction).

analyses.result_summary: OLS with interaction: pembrolizumab effect in MSI-high = -0.265 mo, in MSI-stable = +0.075 mo; interaction coefficient = -0.340 (p=1.53e-01). Group sizes n(pembro+,MSI+)=79, n(pembro+,MSI-)=2305, n(pembro-,MSI+)=1449, n(pembro-,MSI-)=46167.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In PSMA-high patients (psma_high=1), receiving Lu-177-PSMA radioligand therapy (treatment_lu177_psma=1) is associated with longer pfs_months than not receiving it; the magnitude of this PFS gain is larger in PSMA-high than in PSMA-low patients (positive interaction).

analyses.result_summary: OLS with interaction: Lu177-PSMA effect in PSMA-high = -0.011 mo, in PSMA-low = +0.006 mo; interaction coefficient = -0.017 (p=7.37e-01).

proposed_hypotheses.text: In AR-V7 positive patients (ar_v7_positive=1), enzalutamide (treatment_enzalutamide=1) is less effective at prolonging pfs_months than in AR-V7 negative patients (negative interaction term: enzalutamide:ar_v7_positive < 0).
In AR-V7 positive patients, abiraterone (treatment_abiraterone=1) is also less effective at prolonging pfs_months than in AR-V7 negative patients (negative interaction term).

analyses.result_summary: OLS interaction: enzalutamide effect in AR-V7+ = -0.024 mo, in AR-V7- = -0.027 mo; interaction = +0.002 (p=9.57e-01).
OLS interaction: abiraterone effect in AR-V7+ = +0.032 mo, in AR-V7- = -0.015 mo; interaction = +0.048 (p=3.34e-01).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher ECOG performance status score (ecog_ps, range 0-4) is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).

analyses.result_summary: ECOG slope = -1.161 months/unit ECOG (p=0.00e+00). Negative slope is consistent with worse PFS in poor-performance patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher serum PSA (psa_ng_ml) at baseline is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).
Higher serum albumin (albumin_g_dl) is associated with longer pfs_months (positive slope).
Greater 6-month weight loss percent (weight_loss_pct_6mo) is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).

analyses.result_summary: Weight-loss slope = -0.0749 mo per percentage point (p=9.91e-226).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer (mcrpc=1) have shorter pfs_months than non-mCRPC patients (negative effect).
Patients with visceral metastases (visceral_mets=1) have shorter pfs_months than those without visceral mets (negative effect).
Patients with liver metastases (liver_mets=1) have shorter pfs_months than those without liver mets (negative effect).
Patients with bone metastases (bone_mets=1) have shorter pfs_months than those without bone mets (negative effect).

analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS mcrpc+ vs - = 3.509 vs 4.031; diff=-0.521 mo (Welch t-test p=1.58e-181; n+=27481, n-=22519).
Mean PFS visceral_mets+ vs - = 3.706 vs 3.754; diff=-0.048 mo (Welch t-test p=3.45e-02; n+=9973, n-=40027).
Mean PFS liver_mets+ vs - = 3.743 vs 3.744; diff=-0.002 mo (Welch t-test p=9.48e-01; n+=7392, n-=42608).
Mean PFS bone_mets+ vs - = 3.753 vs 3.742; diff=+0.011 mo (Welch t-test p=6.24e-01; n+=10056, n-=39944).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher Gleason score (gleason_score, range 6-10) is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).

analyses.result_summary: Gleason slope = -0.0062 mo per Gleason point (p=4.86e-01).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher LDH (ldh_u_l) is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).
Higher CRP (crp_mg_l) is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).
Higher neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio (nlr) is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).
Higher alkaline phosphatase (alkaline_phosphatase_u_l) is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).
After adjusting for ECOG, age, PSA, albumin, weight loss, mCRPC and visceral mets, treatment_enzalutamide (=1) is associated with longer pfs_months (positive coefficient).
After adjusting for ECOG, age, PSA, albumin, weight loss, mCRPC and visceral mets, treatment_abiraterone (=1) is associated with longer pfs_months (positive coefficient).
After adjusting for ECOG, age, PSA, albumin, weight loss, mCRPC and visceral mets, treatment_docetaxel (=1) is associated with longer pfs_months (positive coefficient).
After adjusting for ECOG, age, PSA, albumin, weight loss, mCRPC and visceral mets, treatment_olaparib (=1) is associated with longer pfs_months (positive coefficient).
After adjusting for ECOG, age, PSA, albumin, weight loss, mCRPC and visceral mets, treatment_lu177_psma (=1) is associated with longer pfs_months (positive coefficient).
After adjusting for ECOG, age, PSA, albumin, weight loss, mCRPC and visceral mets, treatment_pembrolizumab (=1) is associated with longer pfs_months (positive coefficient).

analyses.code: ols('pfs_months ~ treatment_enzalutamide + treatment_abiraterone + treatment_docetaxel + treatment_olaparib + treatment_lu177_psma + treatment_pembrolizumab + ecog_ps + age_years + psa_ng_ml + albumin_g_dl + weight_loss_pct_6mo + mcrpc + visceral_mets', data=df).fit()  # coefficient on treatment_enzalutamide

ols('pfs_months ~ treatment_enzalutamide + treatment_abiraterone + treatment_docetaxel + treatment_olaparib + treatment_lu177_psma + treatment_pembrolizumab + ecog_ps + age_years + psa_ng_ml + albumin_g_dl + weight_loss_pct_6mo + mcrpc + visceral_mets', data=df).fit()  # coefficient on treatment_abiraterone

ols('pfs_months ~ treatment_enzalutamide + treatment_abiraterone + treatment_docetaxel + treatment_olaparib + treatment_lu177_psma + treatment_pembrolizumab + ecog_ps + age_years + psa_ng_ml + albumin_g_dl + weight_loss_pct_6mo + mcrpc + visceral_mets', data=df).fit()  # coefficient on treatment_docetaxel

ols('pfs_months ~ treatment_enzalutamide + treatment_abiraterone + treatment_docetaxel + treatment_olaparib + treatment_lu177_psma + treatment_pembrolizumab + ecog_ps + age_years + psa_ng_ml + albumin_g_dl + weight_loss_pct_6mo + mcrpc + visceral_mets', data=df).fit()  # coefficient on treatment_olaparib

ols('pfs_months ~ treatment_enzalutamide + treatment_abiraterone + treatment_docetaxel + treatment_olaparib + treatment_lu177_psma + treatment_pembrolizumab + ecog_ps + age_years + psa_ng_ml + albumin_g_dl + weight_loss_pct_6mo + mcrpc + visceral_mets', data=df).fit()  # coefficient on treatment_lu177_psma

ols('pfs_months ~ treatment_enzalutamide + treatment_abiraterone + treatment_docetaxel + treatment_olaparib + treatment_lu177_psma + treatment_pembrolizumab + ecog_ps + age_years + psa_ng_ml + albumin_g_dl + weight_loss_pct_6mo + mcrpc + visceral_mets', data=df).fit()  # coefficient on treatment_pembrolizumab

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for ECOG, age, PSA, albumin, weight loss, mCRPC, visceral mets and other treatments, the olaparib x BRCA2-mutation interaction remains positive — i.e., olaparib disproportionately benefits BRCA2-mutant patients.

analyses.code: ols('pfs_months ~ treatment_olaparib*brca2_mutation + clinical covariates + other treatments', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted interaction coefficient (treatment_olaparib:brca2_mutation) = +1.4396 mo (p=0.00e+00); main effect of olaparib in BRCA2- patients = -0.0031, main effect of BRCA2 = +0.0033.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher fatigue grade (fatigue_grade) is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).
Higher pain (pain_nrs) is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).
Higher dyspnea grade (dyspnea_grade) is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).
Higher appetite-loss grade (appetite_loss_grade) is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).

analyses.result_summary: Pain slope = -0.0009 mo per NRS unit (p=7.97e-01).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher number of prior lines of therapy (prior_lines_of_therapy) is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).
Patients who received prior chemotherapy (prior_chemotherapy=1) have shorter pfs_months than those who did not (negative effect).
Patients who received prior immunotherapy (prior_immunotherapy=1) have shorter pfs_months than those who did not (negative effect).

Older age (age_years) is associated with longer pfs_months (positive slope), even after adjusting for ECOG, PSA, mCRPC, albumin and visceral mets — likely reflecting selection of fitter, less aggressive disease in older patients.

analyses.result_summary: Unadjusted age slope = +0.1740 mo per year (p=0.00e+00).

analyses.code: ols('pfs_months ~ age_years + ecog_ps + psa_ng_ml + mcrpc + albumin_g_dl + visceral_mets', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted age slope = +0.1735 mo per year (p=0.00e+00).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher hemoglobin (hemoglobin_g_dl) is associated with longer pfs_months (positive slope).
Most pharmacogenomic SNPs (snp_rs* columns) have no main effect on pfs_months; the proportion significant at p<0.05 in single-SNP regressions does not exceed the chance rate of 5%.

analyses.code: for snp in snp_rs*: ols('pfs_months ~ snp', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Tested 25 SNPs; 1 (4.0%) reached p<0.05; smallest p was snp_rs4986893 at slope=-0.0921 (p=1.45e-02).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months differs across race_ethnicity categories (omnibus F-test).
Mean pfs_months differs across insurance_type categories (omnibus F-test).

analyses.result_summary: Race/ethnicity F-test p=6.89e-01. Group-mean range = 0.060 mo across 5 categories.
Insurance type F-test p=7.94e-01. Group-mean range = 0.028 mo across 4 categories.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within BRCA2-mutant patients only, olaparib (treatment_olaparib=1) yields longer pfs_months than no olaparib (positive within-subgroup effect).
Within BRCA2-wildtype patients only, olaparib has no significant effect on pfs_months (effect near zero).

analyses.code: Welch t-test of pfs_months by treatment_olaparib within brca2_mutation==1
analyses.result_summary: BRCA2+ subgroup: olaparib+ mean PFS=5.216 (n=529); olaparib- mean PFS=3.671 (n=4467); diff=+1.545 mo (p=3.95e-27).

analyses.code: Welch t-test of pfs_months by treatment_olaparib within brca2_mutation==0
analyses.result_summary: BRCA2- subgroup: olaparib+ mean PFS=3.668 (n=4569); olaparib- mean PFS=3.742 (n=40435); diff=-0.073 mo (p=1.91e-02).

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable OLS adjusting for clinical covariates, the treatment_olaparib x brca2_mutation interaction has the predicted sign (positive for olaparib x BRCA2 and pembrolizumab x MSI-high; the lu177 x PSMA interaction's sign is hypothesized to be positive).

In a multivariable OLS adjusting for clinical covariates, the treatment_pembrolizumab x msi_high interaction has the predicted sign (positive for olaparib x BRCA2 and pembrolizumab x MSI-high; the lu177 x PSMA interaction's sign is hypothesized to be positive).

In a multivariable OLS adjusting for clinical covariates, the treatment_lu177_psma x psma_high interaction has the predicted sign (positive for olaparib x BRCA2 and pembrolizumab x MSI-high; the lu177 x PSMA interaction's sign is hypothesized to be positive).

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted interaction coefficient on treatment_olaparib:brca2_mutation = +1.4390 mo (p=0.00e+00).
Adjusted interaction coefficient on treatment_pembrolizumab:msi_high = +0.0489 mo (p=3.51e-01).
Adjusted interaction coefficient on treatment_lu177_psma:psma_high = +0.0005 mo (p=9.65e-01).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with heart_failure=1 have shorter pfs_months than those with heart_failure=0 (negative effect).
Patients with chronic_kidney_disease=1 have shorter pfs_months than those with chronic_kidney_disease=0 (negative effect).
Patients with copd=1 have shorter pfs_months than those with copd=0 (negative effect).
Patients with coronary_artery_disease=1 have shorter pfs_months than those with coronary_artery_disease=0 (negative effect).
Patients with diabetes_mellitus=1 have shorter pfs_months than those with diabetes_mellitus=0 (negative effect).
Patients with hypertension=1 have shorter pfs_months than those with hypertension=0 (negative effect).
Patients with atrial_fibrillation=1 have shorter pfs_months than those with atrial_fibrillation=0 (negative effect).
Patients in rural residence (rural_residence=1) have shorter pfs_months than urban patients (negative effect).
More years of education (education_years) is associated with longer pfs_months (positive slope).
Greater smoking pack-years (smoking_pack_years) is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).

analyses.result_summary: Slope = -0.0025 mo per year of education (p=4.02e-01).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with tp53_mutation=1 have shorter pfs_months than those without (negative effect).
Patients with pten_loss=1 have shorter pfs_months than those without (negative effect).
Patients with cdkn2a_loss=1 have shorter pfs_months than those without (negative effect).
In BRCA2-mutant mCRPC patients, olaparib improves pfs_months relative to no olaparib (positive within-subgroup effect).
In BRCA2-mutant non-mCRPC patients, olaparib also improves pfs_months relative to no olaparib (positive within-subgroup effect).

analyses.code: Welch t-test in BRCA2+ & mCRPC stratum
analyses.result_summary: BRCA2+/mCRPC: olap+ n=296 mean=4.945; olap- n=2428 mean=3.397; diff=+1.548 (p=7.91e-16).

analyses.code: Welch t-test in BRCA2+ & non-mCRPC stratum
analyses.result_summary: BRCA2+/non-mCRPC: olap+ n=233 mean=5.561; olap- n=2039 mean=3.997; diff=+1.564 (p=2.41e-13).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within MSI-high patients only, pembrolizumab (treatment_pembrolizumab=1) is associated with longer pfs_months than no pembrolizumab (positive within-subgroup effect).
Within PSMA-high patients only, Lu-177-PSMA (treatment_lu177_psma=1) is associated with longer pfs_months than no Lu-177-PSMA (positive within-subgroup effect).

analyses.result_summary: MSI-high: pembro+ n=79 mean=3.527; pembro- n=1449 mean=3.792; diff=-0.265 (p=2.39e-01).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within enzalutamide-treated patients, AR-V7 positive status is associated with shorter pfs_months than AR-V7 negative status (negative effect).

A multivariable OLS combining the strongest predictors (age, ECOG, PSA, albumin, weight loss, mCRPC, visceral/liver/bone mets, Gleason, TP53, PTEN, hemoglobin, LDH, CRP, NLR, ALP, symptom grades, prior lines, treatments and key predictive interactions) explains a substantial fraction of variance in pfs_months (adjusted R^2 > 0.70 expected given the strong age signal).

analyses.code: Welch t-test of pfs_months by ar_v7_positive within treatment_enzalutamide==1
analyses.result_summary: Enzalutamide-treated AR-V7+ vs AR-V7-: 3.719 vs 3.731 mo; diff=-0.011 (p=7.56e-01).
Multivariable model adjusted R^2 = 0.9518; F p-value = 0.00e+00; coefficient on treatment_olaparib:brca2_mutation = +1.4365 (p=0.00e+00).
